purpose: Determine whether fine query-by-hyperedge reweighting has a larger viable action space than the rejected B2.1 whole-map router, and whether a conservative train-only static sparse suppression patch transfers at all. These are posthoc development diagnostics, not preregistered results.
oracle_definition: For each query, keep the frozen MERGED top-20 candidate set and permit a gold oracle to reorder it before recall@10. Headroom equals oracle recall@10 minus frozen MERGED recall@10. No candidate outside top-20 can enter, so this is a lower bound on the full-candidate gold reranking ceiling and an upper bound on any executable policy restricted to the same top-20.

train_only_static_sparse_suppression_pro.definition: Fit at most 32 query-independent edge-ID attenuations on train only. An edge is eligible when it appears as non-gold in the frozen MERGED top-20 for at least three independent train components and is never train gold. Levels are log(0.9), log(0.7), and log(0.5), applied with slow_scale=0.15. Greedy selection maximizes train recall@10 with deterministic least-magnitude and lexical tie breaks; the unchanged patch is then read on calibration and test.
train_only_static_sparse_suppression_pro.summary: All six calibration and all six test deltas are exactly 0.0. This rejects only this edge-identity greedy transfer rule as a useful next confirmatory learner; it does not reject all possible slow salience learning.

limitations: The B2.1 train, calibration, and test cohorts have already been inspected and are development-only for B2.2; none is a fresh confirmatory holdout.
Gold-oracle headroom does not show that observable features can identify the useful edge-level reordering.
The v1 scorepacks retain only top-20 IDs and final scores, not full edge-query score components, so they cannot prove exact full-candidate SemanticWeight reranking.
The static probe is one conservative edge-identity learner over a truncated top-20 buffer; its zero transfer is not a global oracle for every query-independent salience function.
diagnostic_conclusion: The failure of B2.1 is specific to coarse whole-map routing; fine bond-level reordering has more than the +0.02 target headroom on both primary development cohorts.

A conservative static edge-ID suppression rule produces small train gains but exactly zero calibration/test gain in all six seed-dataset cells, so it should not be promoted into a full confirmatory branch.

The next development variable should be a learned query-conditioned bond potential over a frozen candidate set, with durable slow salience kept separate and distilled only after repeated cross-query evidence.

Before confirmation, compile a full-candidate score-component pack and prove neutral-weight exact reproduction; then freeze the learner and evaluate on a newly sealed benchmark such as a fresh PhantomWiki universe.
